summary: Mission Control storage ports and filesystem adapters are correctly implemented. All 4 adapters (Mission, Feature, Assertion, Checkpoint) follow the port interfaces, use atomic file operations via lib/fs.ts utilities, and support the expected storage layout. Service wiring properly exposes all new ports through getServices(). Test mocks in tests/helpers/mocks.ts cover all new store ports. The .maestro/missions/ directory is properly gitignored.

codeReviewIssues.description: FsAssertionStoreAdapter uses a single assertions.json file per mission, which differs from the 'one JSON file per feature' pattern. While functionally acceptable, this inconsistency in storage strategy between features and assertions could be addressed for architectural uniformity.

FsCheckpointStoreAdapter.generateCheckpointId() uses millisecond-precision timestamps. While collisions are unlikely in practice, consider adding a random suffix or counter if concurrent checkpoint creation becomes a requirement.
Missing dedicated test file for assertion-store.adapter.ts. The feature-store adapter has mission-store.adapter.test.ts, but assertion-store.adapter lacks corresponding unit tests.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Services are properly wired in src/services.ts with all 4 new adapters (FsMissionStoreAdapter, FsFeatureStoreAdapter, FsAssertionStoreAdapter, FsCheckpointStoreAdapter) initialized in initServices() and exposed through the Services interface.
sharedStateObservations.recommendation: No changes needed. Service wiring is complete and follows the established pattern.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The implementation follows AGENTS.md TypeScript style guidelines: interfaces for object shapes, explicit return types on public functions, readonly for immutable data, and arrow functions for callbacks in test mocks.
sharedStateObservations.recommendation: No changes needed. Code style is consistent with project guidelines.
